description_long: This is a vintage-style portable record player housed in a textured, dark brown rectangular case with a hinged lid that opens vertically. The turntable platter is centrally located and holds a black vinyl record with a red label, while a metallic tonearm with a yellow handle extends over the record. The case features a visible latch at the front and a winding key on the side, suggesting a manual or wind-up mechanism. The surface of the case appears to be covered in a fabric or faux-leather material, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The interior is molded to fit the turntable components, and the lid is plain with no visible branding or embellishments. The overall design is compact, making it suitable for tabletop or portable use in urban environments.
description: A vintage portable record player in a textured brown case with a hinged lid, central turntable, metallic tonearm, and side winding key.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid, central turntable with a record, metallic tonearm, and front latch.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the winding key, the profile of the tonearm, and the depth of the open lid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, textured back of the case and the protruding winding key.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tonearm's position over the record and the open lid's thickness.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and centrally located within the case. It is black, likely made of plastic or metal, and holds a vinyl record with a red label. The platter is slightly raised above the base and appears smooth, with no visible grooves or patterns on its surface.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the right side of the case over the record. It is medium-length, silver in color, and has a yellow handle or knob at the end. The tonearm is positioned above the platter, suggesting it is ready to play or be moved into place.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its location is implied at the end of the tonearm near the record's surface. It appears to be a small, separate component attached to the tonearm.
attribute_control_knobs: A single winding key is visible on the side of the case, yellow in color and likely made of plastic or metal. No other control knobs, switches, or buttons are visible.
attribute_dust_cover: The dust cover is present in the form of a hinged, opaque lid made of the same textured brown material as the case. It is open and attached at the back with visible hinges, and does not appear to be transparent or removable.
